channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: A Europe of clean, green cities and resurgent industry is a fantasy – unless we get really creative | Hans Larsson
item.description: “Bitterfeld, Bitterfeld, where dirt falls from the sky,” went a popular saying.

item.title: A delightful day at the dump: ‘The trick is not to leave with more stuff than I arrived with!’
item.description: When an embalmed rabbit in a Perspex box arrived at the dump in Chingford, north-east London, last year, with fur on its head but its organs and skeleton exposed to teach veterinary students about the digestive system, Lisa Charlton knew she had to save it from landfill.

item.title: Government's controversial freedom of information reforms dropped after broad opposition
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: 'Slap in the face': Former Tropfest winner slams festival for AI film inclusion
item.description: Filmmakers have slammed the selection of a completely AI-generated film as a finalist at the recently returned Tropfest short film festival.

item.title: Nepal votes in election pitting entrenched old guard against a powerful youth movement
item.description: Nearly six months after a wave of unprecedented youth-led protests forced Nepal’s then prime minister to quit, people have began voting in a general election that is shaping up as a high-stakes showdown between the entrenched old guard and a powerful youth movement.

item.title: ‘A win for democracy’: Labor abandons freedom of information law changes that would have reduced transparency
item.description: The Labor government has dumped its controversial changes to the freedom of information request system, which would have imposed new fees and further reduced transparency, after admitting it had no pathway to passing the parliament.

item.title: TerraPower gets OK to start construction of its first nuclear plant
item.description: Plant won’t be done until 2030 at the earliest, and it still needs an operating license.

item.title: Arsenal go seven points clear with Bukayo Saka on target in tough Brighton win
item.description: Arsenal did not come to see the ­seaside.

item.title: Aaron Judge and a loaded US roster hope to dethrone Ohtani’s Japan at the World Baseball Classic
item.description: Aaron Judge is trading pinstripes for a plain jersey with red, white and blue letters.

item.title: Major search for 18-year-old swimmer lost from popular Sunshine Coast beach
item.description: A major search along popular Sunshine Coast beaches has restarted this morning after an 18-year-old swimmer never returned to the sand overnight.

item.title: João Pedro hat-trick fires Chelsea to emphatic comeback win at Aston Villa
item.description: As these teams emerged for kick-off, the Holte End displayed a tifo proudly flaunting Aston Villa’s hand, chiefly an ace of clubs.

item.title: Why Iran's most powerful ally is not coming to its aid
item.description: President Donald Trump's backers have celebrated the United States and Israel's attack on Iran as a blow for not just the Iranian regime, but also for China.

item.title: Can China's 'Steel Roses' shine bright once again?
item.description: For decades, China's women's national football team have done what the men never could: win.

item.title: The best beginner pickleball gear, according to pros
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Newcastle United v Manchester United: Premier League – live
item.description: A reminder of where both teams lie going into tonight’s five-match card.

item.title: Colossal Biosciences breeds controversy while trying to revive mammoths
item.description: When the elevator doors part on the second floor of a two-story brick and glass building in an office park on the outskirts of downtown Dallas, it feels like a portal opening to a different world.

item.title: How did radio's most successful duo end here?
item.description: I don't think Andrew anticipated the door opening might be that of a police station, and the door closing would be his own front door — behind him.

item.title: In these troubled times, who isn’t loving the nostalgia of the Kennedys and Love Story’s 90s New York? | Emma Brockes
item.description: If you are looking for a break in the clouds from this terrible news cycle, can I direct you towards Love Story, the nine-part series executive-produced – but crucially, not written! – by Ryan Murphy, which documents the love and untimely deaths of John F Kennedy Jr and his wife, Carolyn Bessette.

item.title: Greetings from Southwest Papua, which has some of the world's richest marine biodiversity
item.description: Far-Flung Postcards is a weekly series in which NPR's international team shares moments from their lives and work around the world.

item.title: Dennis Cometti was erudite, funny and engaging. His witticisms could fill a library
item.description: The late Tony Charlton, who called a dozen VFL grand finals and three Olympic Games, said sporting commentators should “produce words like bubbles in champagne”.

item.title: ‘Apartheid newsroom’: minority ethnic journalists still locked out of top jobs, report finds
item.description: Broadcast journalists from ethnic minorities are still locked out of top jobs and face a backlash after being perceived as “diversity hires”, according to a survey of UK television newsrooms.

item.title: 'Overjoyed': Emotions run high as first flight out of Middle East lands in Sydney
item.description: About 200 Australians have landed safely in Sydney after flying out of Dubai following the outbreak of America and Israel's war with Iran.

item.title: Why supporting a shelter for women is now 'kind of radioactive'
item.description: There's one voice Lisseth can't get out of her head — the pleas of a 22-year-old woman.

item.title: Nvidia and UK Wealth Fund invest in British autonomous driving startup Oxa
item.description: Nvidia is investing in the British autonomous driving startup Oxa, alongside backing from the UK’s National Wealth Fund, in a boost to the country’s technology sector.

item.title: David and Victoria Beckham wish Brooklyn happy birthday despite feud
item.description: Sir David and Lady Beckham have wished son Brooklyn a happy birthday on Instagram, two months after the 27-year-old exposed a rift in the family.

item.title: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba favourite to succeed him as Iran’s supreme leader
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the assassinated Ali Khamenei, is being heavily tipped to succeed his father as supreme leader of Iran, which would pitch a hardliner into the task of steering the Islamic republic through the most turbulent period in its 48-year history and offer a powerful signal that, for now, it has no intention of changing course.

item.title: ‘I owe Iron Maiden my English A-level!’ The great literature our writers discovered through pop music
item.description: Ahead of World Book Day on Thursday, Guardian music writers pick out the musicians whose literary references illuminated them – from Adam Ant on Joe Orton to the National on Grace PaleyI first heard the Cure’s Charlotte Sometimes as a teenager, and it was like waking up from a dream.

item.title: Talarico wins Texas Democratic primary as Republicans face runoff – US politics live
item.description: As the war in the Middle East rages on half a world away, the midterm primary season kicked off with James Talarico winning the Democratic nomination for a US Senate seat in Texas – and a Republican runoff.

item.title: The great shared bed debate is here – and you have to pick a side | Polly Hudson
item.description: How well do any of us really know our friends? You may be confident of the answer to that, feel secure – smug, even – but be warned.

item.title: Would you and your sexual partner like to share the story of what you get up to in the bedroom?
item.description: How often do you have sex? The Guardian is looking for couples to talk honestly – and completely anonymously – about what they get up to in the bedroom for the Saturday magazine’s much-loved This is How We Do It column. The idea behind the column is to provide a counterpoint to the airbrushed, exaggerated stories about sex we see on TV and in the media.

item.title: GOP heads to runoff; Democrat Talarico wins big in Texas primary
item.description: Democrat James Talarico beat Rep.

item.title: Outgunned review – action-thriller in Angola outstays its welcome
item.description: Here is an action-thriller that opens with some zesty Call of Duty-style military violence unfolding in Angola in 2013.

item.title: James Talarico wins Democratic primary for US Senate in Texas
item.description: James Talarico won the Democratic nomination for a US Senate seat in Texas on Tuesday, capping a remarkable rise from state lawmaker and seminary student to the party’s standard-bearer in one of the key races of the 2026 midterm cycle.

item.title: Why Sinners should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: It’s a symptom of the modern entertainment landscape that movies are now either commercially successful or critically acclaimed, but rarely both.

item.title: 'I've lost something truly precious': McAvaney mourns great mate Cometti
item.description: For decades, the greatest moments in Australian rules football were described and embellished by Dennis Cometti and Bruce McAvaney, forming one of the great double acts.

item.title: Liam Rosenior knows clock is ticking on Chelsea’s chance of Champions League spot
item.description: It is natural for young people to feel they have all the time in the world.

item.title: Horizons and highways: Franco Fontana’s stunning photographic experiments – in pictures
item.description: A mesmerising new exhibition showcases the work of the Italian colour pioneer whose landscapes, motorways and swimming pools often seem more like abstract paintings Continue reading.

item.title: ‘That thrush just did something incredible’: tuning in to bird calls on a North York Moors walk
item.description: We follow a path up to an open ridge, but bird sounds are conspicuously absent.

item.title: A moment that changed me: my girlfriend criticised my kisses – and it led to the best decision of my life
item.description: She said kissing me was like licking an ashtray, and I knew I had to quit smoking.

item.title: Protect caterpillars as UK’s moth population plummets, urge charities
item.description: As spring unfolds and plants come to life, gardeners often fight a losing battle against the caterpillars who munch their cabbages.

item.title: Pregnant women shed grey matter to help with motherhood, study seen by BBC suggests
item.description: "Baby brain" is a cliche long-used to describe women becoming forgetful and feeling less capable during pregnancy.

item.title: Can a robot recruiter really spot a good carer?
item.description: Just half an hour after she applied for a care job, Mollie Cole-Wilkin's phone rang.

item.title: The fascinating science of dreams, and why some of us don't remember them
item.description: Some mornings, you wake up and the dream is right there.

item.title: First flight has left Dubai for Sydney. How do airlines determine it is safe?
item.description: A flight from Dubai to Sydney has departed the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

item.title: Melbourne F1 Grand Prix rebuild sees $115m blowout due to asbestos
item.description: The cost of redeveloping Melbourne's Grand Prix pit building has blown out by $115 million due to asbestos clean-up requirements.

item.title: Winter Paralympics 2026: who are Australia’s top medal contenders? | Kieran Pender
item.description: Following a Winter Olympics of unprecedented success for team Australia, the nation’s para-athletes will be hoping to emulate that golden form when the Milano Cortina Paralympics begin on Friday.

item.title: Dennis Cometti, Australian sports commentary great, dies aged 76
item.description: Dennis Cometti, one of the greats of Australian sports commentary, has died at the age of 76.

item.title: Breaking: Legendary sports commentator Dennis Cometti dies
item.description: Legendary sports commentator Dennis Cometti has died aged 76, his family has confirmed.

item.title: North Carolina: Roy Cooper and Michael Whatley win primaries to set up Senate contest
item.description: North Carolina’s competitive Senate race came into shape on Tuesday, with former Democratic governor Roy Cooper and former Republican National Committee chair Michael Whatley winning their respective primaries.

item.title: Celebrating Holi - the Hindu festival brings its array of colours to India and elsewhere
item.description: Millions of people are celebrating Holi, the festival of colours, in India and across the world.

item.title: Red alert: the best photos of the rare blood moon total lunar eclipse – in pictures
item.description: A rare total lunar eclipse on Tuesday night resulted in a spectacular blood moon, seen first in Western Australia, then in parts of Asia, Europe and across North America.

item.title: Can’t win? Drop out: California Democrats plead with governor hopefuls as party fears Republican surge
item.description: It’s been three decades since Democrats last had a wide open contest for the California governorship, one of the most visible and most powerful positions in the US.

item.title: Talarico wins Democratic nomination for Texas Senate seat; Republicans set for runoff
item.description: The outlines of the race for a U.S.

item.title: Event planners of Barunga Festival 'blindsided' by hiring of new agency
item.description: The company that has managed the planning of the Northern Territory's iconic Barunga Festival for the past three years will not have its contract renewed this year, in a change in direction for the event.

item.title: Premier League top scorers 2025-26: who is leading race for golden boot?
item.description: See which hot shots are leading the way in the English top flight’s goalscoring charts this season Continue reading.

item.title: Live: Australian share market tumbles as economic growth picks up
item.description: The Australian share market has fallen sharply as worries about a drawn-out Middle East war, surging oil prices and potentially higher inflation are fresh on investors' minds.

item.title: Alessia Russo double for England sparks Women’s World Cup qualifying win against Ukraine
item.description: Stressed, anxious or doubtful after a goalless first half? Not these ­England players, who remained fully confident in their ability and found a flurry of ­second-half goals to begin their ­Women’s World Cup ­qualifying ­campaign with a 6-1 win against Ukraine.

item.title: 'Hope' for drought-hit farmers after best rain in six months
item.description: Widespread rain across central and southern New South Wales has resulted in some falls of more than 140 millimetres.

item.title: Tourists crowding an echidna reveal the problem facing this popular island
item.description: With tourist numbers rapidly rising on Maria Island, authorities are working out the best way to accommodate them.

item.title: High-tech snowplows and AI help cities clean up from big storms
item.description: Residents of Syracuse, New York — America’s snowiest city — once barraged a service hotline with street neglect complaints during blizzards, even if plows had passed two hours earlier but the work was hidden by fresh snow.

item.title: ‘People are gripped by huge fear’: defiance and hope on the streets of Tehran
item.description: Fear, defiance, and quiet celebration intermingled in Tehran with everyday chores, local people said, as Iran’s capital continued to be rocked by American and Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Declutter, define, discard: seven easy and cheap home organization tips, from experts
item.description: Overcurated home organization content has flourished on social media for the past decade: well-lit photos of pantries, closets and bathrooms with contents arranged in clear acrylic bins.

item.title: Ruth Langsford: Counselling is the best thing I ever did
item.description: The TV presenter shares how counselling has given her new perspectives on life.

item.title: ‘Cheap as chips and save backache’: gardening pros on the tools they can’t live without
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: How AI is being used to support positive mental health
item.description: Emma-Louise Amanshia speaks to a mental health clinician about the Wysa AI chatbot.

item.title: Countess Luann: ‘One lesson I wish I’d learned earlier? If you don’t think you’re great, nobody else will’
item.description: Luann, money can’t buy you class.

item.title: AFL 2026 predicted ladder part three: Brisbane’s best may be yet to come
item.description: “It’s definitely not ideal, is it?” Darcy Fogarty said in the days following Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

item.title: Sea moss is everywhere on TikTok – yes it’s natural but that doesn’t mean it’s safe or beneficial for all | Antiviral
item.description: If you have a weak stomach, I don’t recommend watching one of the many videos on social media of influencers dipping their spoons into a jar of sea moss and putting mounds of the congealed substance – with a texture resembling congealed oil or snot – straight into their mouths.

item.title: Spring forecast: Reeves insists Labour has ‘right economic plan’ as 2026 growth downgraded
item.description: Rachel Reeves insisted Labour has “the right economic plan” for a world that has become “yet more uncertain” as she delivered a spring forecast that downgraded growth for this year.

item.title: Schools are using AI counselors to track students’ mental health. Is it safe?
item.description: Brittani Phillips checked her phone.

item.title: Five of the most interesting upcoming indie games
item.description: These days, it’s easy to fall into the trap of thinking that every new indie game is either a co-op extraction shooter or a roguelike deck-builder – fortunately that’s not quite the case.

item.title: Baftas host Alan Cumming criticises BBC for ‘broadcasting slurs and censoring free speech’
item.description: Alan Cumming has joined the chorus of disapproval at the BBC’s failure to edit out a racial slur from their Baftas telecast, saying it turned “what should have been an evening celebrating diversity and inclusion into a trauma-triggering shitshow”.

item.title: LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users at scale with surprising accuracy
item.description: Pseudonymity has never been perfect for preserving privacy.

item.title: Richard Osman quits quiz show House of Games after 'amazing nine years'
item.description: Richard Osman's House of Games will be getting a new name after its current host announced he is leaving the BBC quiz show.

item.title: Showdown over datacenter politics at heart of North Carolina primary
item.description: A North Carolina congressional primary on Tuesday is an early test of datacenter politics – a fight increasingly shaping elections nationwide.

item.title: ‘Where the magic really happens’: the influencers out to celebrate – and save – Britain’s ‘proper boozers’
item.description: The Calthorpe Arms on Gray’s Inn Road is a fairly atypical central London pub.

item.title: Family GP had 'sexual motivation' for breast and vaginal exams, tribunal finds
item.description: One of Stellios (Stan) Theodoros's former patients, Beverley*, told the ABC she hoped the decision sent a "strong and clear message".

item.title: My sexual freedom odyssey: what ancient African wisdom can teach us about pleasure today
item.description: In the kitchen of my Airbnb in Dar es Salaam I stripped down to my underwear and wrapped a colourful kanga cloth around my hips.

item.title: Why Frankenstein should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: Guillermo del Toro has spent his career humanising monsters, once calling them the “patron saints of our blissful imperfection”, so his adaptation of Frankenstein was always going to be a match made in heaven.

item.title: Want a better job and a pay rise? Eleven ways to progress at work – and avoid a ‘dry promotion’
item.description: There is nothing worse than feeling stuck in a job.

item.title: ‘Cultural monstrosities!’ The thrilling visual legacy of punk and post-punk – in pictures
item.description: From bold anti-Nazi posters to an acid-drenched take on Jean Cocteau, a new exhibition, curated by writer Philip Hoare, shows how influential the DIY designs of the 70s and 80s became Continue reading.

item.title: Australia news live: Kyle and Jackie O’s breakfast radio show on KIIS taken off-air effective immediately
item.description: ARN Media has just announced that both Kyle Sandilands and Jackie “O” Henderson will cease to present the Kiis FM breakfast show, on the basis that Henderson has expressed she “cannot continue to work” with Sandilands.

item.title: 'Our heart goes out to them': Matildas star lauds Iran players' bravery
item.description: Matildas star Amy Sayer has lauded the bravery of Iran's players ahead of this week's Asian Cup clash between the two sides.

item.title: Farmers hit 'ambitious' $100b target years ahead of schedule
item.description: Australia's peak farming body is celebrating the industry's achievement of reaching its $100 billion goal.

item.title: Broome doesn't have accessible taxis, so one local is providing free rides
item.description: With no wheelchair-accessible taxi services available, Andy Guidera has been providing free lifts to his community in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Territory Labor never should have lost Nightcliff. Does it deserve to win it back?
item.description: Nightcliff is the seat Territory Labor should never have lost.

item.title: Jesse Jackson returns to South Carolina to be honored by his home state: ‘He freed us all’
item.description: After a long career of fighting for civil rights, the Rev Jesse Jackson Sr is visiting his home for one last time to lie in state at the South Carolina capitol on Monday.

item.title: Meet the $235,000 bull that dazzled buyers with his good looks
item.description: A South Australian Angus bull that sold at auction for $235,000 has set a state record.

item.title: The greenest flags: virtue signals that help you find love – from patchwork clothes to car sharing
item.description: Age: This is a thing for younger people, so listen up, boomers.

item.title: Yes, relegation is now a very real possibility for Tottenham | Jonathan Wilson
item.description: Last week, after Tottenham had lost 4-1 at home to Arsenal, Igor Tudor was bullish.

item.title: Why a good night's sleep starts in the morning
item.description: Sleep psychologist Stephanie Romiszewski explains the benefits of waking up at the same time every day.

item.title: $599 M4 iPad Air is a lot like the old one, but with a substantial RAM boost
item.description: Unexpected RAM upgrade is the highlight of an otherwise straightforward refresh.

item.title: Rukmini Iyer’s quick and easy recipe for chard borani soup with yoghurt, crispy garlic and beans | Quick and easy
item.description: I am emphatically not a dip person (see also: salad), but the first time I tried chard borani, a Persian dip made with chard and yoghurt, I became so obsessed that we’ve been having it on repeat at home ever since.

item.title: ‘We have to take bold decisions’: Williams chief James Vowles on 2026 hopes and F1 title dreams
item.description: Vowles is one of the most interesting men in F1 and not just because, as the director of strategy, he helped two of his past teams win nine constructors’ championships, including eight drivers’ titles.

item.title: Top UK film and TV production school puts spotlight on students with disabilities
item.description: For a long time, physically disabled students who dreamed of studying at the UK’s most prestigious film and TV production school had nowhere to stay in the local area.

item.title: ‘Do what you really want to do while you’re still alive’: Masayoshi Takanaka, the Japanese guitar hero surfing a second wave in his 70s
item.description: Sadistic Mika Band appeared on the BBC’s music television show the Old Grey Whistle Test during that visit, in a performance Takanaka proudly recalls being praised by fellow guitarist Jeff Beck.

item.title: The one change that worked: I stopped planning holidays – and found the joy in travel
item.description: I have always been indecisive and scared of wasting money.

item.title: Texas primaries could test whether Latino support for GOP is holding after 2024 gains
item.description: Ongoing primary elections in Texas could be a first look at whether Latino swing voters, who are increasingly influential in state elections, are sticking with the Republican Party.

item.title: Why The Secret Agent should win the best picture Oscar
item.description: As ever, this year’s Oscars have their half-dozen or so favourites and frontrunners, some truly outstanding movies among them.

item.title: More than 100,000 Britons register for help in Middle East
item.description: A total of 102,000 Britons have registered their presence in the Middle East with the UK government, as Iranian strikes continue across the region.

item.title: ‘You know when you’ve hit it – it’s a transformation’: Ruth E Carter on building the bold world of Sinners
item.description: This is just a small example of the canny period world-building that has made Carter the most-garlanded Black woman in Oscars history, and the owner of a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame (her family were in attendance, she says, of its Covid-era unveiling, while Oprah and Eddie Murphy dialled in via Zoom).

item.title: Becoming George by Fiona Sampson review – the remarkable story of a cross-dressing 19th century novelist
item.description: If we really are in a reading crisis – whether you blame TikTok or podcasts – it stands to reason that, of all the genres, literary biography might have particular cause to fear for its life: who wants the life story of somebody whose books no one reads?.

item.title: Is 14 the magic number? Promoted trio make instant Super League impact
item.description: When the Super League fixtures were released late last year, it was hard not to be drawn to this weekend.

item.title: ‘I love midges because I know what their hearts look like’: is the passion for taxonomy in danger of dying out?
item.description: Once Art Borkent starts speaking about biting midges, he rarely pauses for breath.

item.title: Bon appétit! Celebrity pals pop round for lunch: best podcasts of the week
item.description: Joanna Page and her Gavin & Stacey on-screen husband Mathew Horne host a foodie podcast for celebs.

item.title: My search for the perfect brown bar in Amsterdam
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Tipping Point contestant who promised to use winnings on IVF now has triplets
item.description: A man who won ITV's Tipping Point says there was "no way" he and his wife would have had their triplets through IVF without the cash prize.

item.title: More than 50,000 people waited over 24 hours in A&E corridor care, BBC investigation finds
item.description: More than 52,000 patients waited longer than 24 hours to be admitted to hospitals across north-west England last year, a BBC investigation has revealed.

item.title: 'I'm excited for new challenge after 16 years on Hollyoaks'
item.description: Spoiler warning: This article reveals details from upcoming episodes of Hollyoaks.

item.title: What to know about Iran’s Revolutionary Guard, a powerful force within the country’s theocracy
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard has grown into a powerful force within the country’s theocracy, answering only to its supreme leader and overseeing its ballistic missile arsenal and launching attacks overseas.

item.title: SA Labor pledges OSHC expansion as Liberals support free school concept
item.description: The SA opposition has flagged its intent to back Labor's election promise to make public schooling free, in a sign the measure is likely to proceed, no matter who forms government.

item.title: Married at Mardi Gras: the Free, Gay n Happy float – in pictures
item.description: No-one could rain on this parade.

item.title: Simple recipes to celebrate autumn and enjoy the cosy kitchen
item.description: Autumn means goodbye to the long days of summer but hello to the kind of weather that lends itself a little more to being inside and cooking.

item.title: When Australians can get the best view of the 'eerie' blood moon
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Who is leading Iran now — and how will a new supreme leader be chosen?
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Delroy Lindo thankful for ‘love and support’ after N-word incident at Baftas
item.description: British-American actor Delroy Lindo expressed gratitude for “the support and love” he and Michael B Jordan have received after a man with Tourette syndrome (TS) shouted the N-word as the two men presented a Bafta award.

item.title: Should tick safety be as popular as 'slip, slop, slap'?
item.description: In early 2025, Queenslander Matt Jacobs couldn't figure out why everything was making him sick.

item.title: Arsenal edge battling Chelsea thanks to Timber and Raya to restore five-point lead
item.description: There were 63 minutes on the Emirates Stadium clock and the Arsenal crowd were in a state of extreme agitation.

item.title: Teeth, finger bones and blessings: Buddhist relics inspire belief
item.description: ROSEMEAD, Calif.

item.title: Brave, visionary and queer: the Bohemian brilliance of author George Sand
item.description: It would be hard to find a more courageous and perverse, iconic yet controversial figure in European literary history than George Sand.

item.title: How to make the perfect bara brith – recipe | Felicity Cloake's How to make the perfect …
item.description: Bara brith, the traditional Welsh fruit loaf whose name means speckled bread, is, as Ben Mervis notes, not dissimilar to Yorkshire brack, Irish barmbrack and Scottish “kerrie loaf” – the last is a new one on me, though, of course, I’m more than familiar with how well they all pair with strong tea and cold salty butter.

item.title: Italian skier Sofia Goggia wins super-G to regain commanding lead in World Cup discipline standings
item.description: SOLDEU, Andorra (AP) — Sofia Goggia won her second super-G of the season Sunday and regained a commanding lead in the race to the World Cup discipline title.

item.title: How succession works in Iran and who could be the country’s next supreme leader
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who assembled theocratic power in Iran over the decades as its supreme leader and sought to turn it into a regional powerhouse, bringing it into confrontation with Israel and the United States over its nuclear program while crushing democracy protesters at home, has died, Iranian state media confirmed early Sunday.

item.title: How the internet reacted to Matildas 1-0 win over Philippines
item.description: The last time the Matildas played the Philippines, they beat them 8-0.

item.title: Sam Kerr header delivers Matildas win over Philippines in Women’s Asian Cup opener
item.description: The Matildas’ Asian Cup campaign is off to an ideal, if rusty, start with a 1-0 defeat of the Philippines in their first group-stage match in Perth on Sunday afternoon.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie may never know what happened to her loved one. In the US, she’s not alone
item.description: Savannah Guthrie could be moving back to New York to resume anchoring NBC’s Today show and acknowledging that her 84-year-old mother, Nancy, may not be found a month after she disappeared from her Tucson, Arizona, home in the middle of the night.

item.title: Demons premiership winner May retires from AFL 'with a heavy heart'
item.description: May was a member of the Demons' 2021 premiership-winning squad.

item.title: Fire pip Lynx in overtime to win WNBL Championship
item.description: Townsville Fire defeated Perth Lynx 108-105 in the second game of the WNBL grand final series.

item.title: British stonemasons win snow sculpting competition in Switzerland
item.description: Two stonemasons have won a snow sculpting prize as part of Team GB's entry to the World Snow Festival in Switzerland.

item.title: ‘By 18 I was having sex to the music of Brian Eno’: Tim Booth’s honest playlist
item.description: The James frontman fell for Leonard Cohen as a child and would do Val Doonican at karaoke.

item.title: Aussie Green hangs on to win Women's World Championship by one shot
item.description: Hannah Green has won the Women's World Championship in Singapore by one stroke.

item.title: Live: Matildas up and running with Asian Cup win over Filipinas
item.description: The Matildas get off to a winning start, but the Filipinas don't make things easy in the opening game of the 2026 Women's Asian Cup at Perth Stadium.

item.title: 'It's joy in a pot': Why houseplants could make you happier
item.description: Jonny Balchandani is so passionate about plants he turned his spare bedroom into a greenhouse.

item.title: Stunning views, honesty shops and community pubs: people power on the Llŷn peninsula in Wales
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Kalyn Ponga inspires Newcastle to Las Vegas NRL win over North Queensland
item.description: Newcastle’s new-look halves pairing has lasted just 25 minutes, but Kalyn Ponga remained the Knights’ most important man in a 28-18 win over North Queensland in Las Vegas.

item.title: My best friend’s ex is turning my partner against her. How can we heal our friendship group? | Annalisa Barbieri
item.description: I’ve been best friends with Ellie [all names have been changed] for more than half my life.

item.title: ‘For us, it’s like Christmas’: Bad Bunny gets rapturous welcome at first-ever Australian show
item.description: Few Australian tours carry the weight of Bad Bunny’s first-ever shows in the country.

item.title: Exhibition shares First Nations stories of 'strength and resilience'
item.description: The Legacies Karlkurla exhibition showcases personal stories of First Nations elders from the Goldfields region.

item.title: This man was abused for appearing on a Welcome to Heathrow poster. Then he met his trolls
item.description: A single social media post turned what had been one of Syed Usman Shah's "proudest moments" into one of the most overwhelming and upsetting.

item.title: How responsible are climbers for each other's safety?
item.description: When Rebekah Lee was in her twenties, she decided to embark on an ambitious climb up Mount Stuart in Washington State and "made a series of bad decisions".

item.title: Survey shows successful reintroduction of eastern quolls to mainland Australia
item.description: An endangered "native cat" is making a celebrated comeback, with a breeding population of eastern quolls making a new home for themselves in a small pocket of south-east mainland Australia.

item.title: Buskers at Australian championships showcase why live is best
item.description: Dozens of musicians have busted out their best, competing in the national busking championships in Stanthorpe, Queensland.

item.title: Sri Lanka helps New Zealand into World Cup semis despite Pakistan defeat
item.description: New Zealand has been left celebrating in the team hotel as it watched Pakistan fall short in its bold bid to pinch a place in the T20 World Cup semifinals.

item.title: 'Timing is impeccable': Record rain for parts of SA
item.description: Record rainfall has been welcomed by some in the Eyre Peninsula.

item.title: Where does all that rain end up? Here, and Lake Eyre watchers are excited
item.description: For the second year in a row, an inland sea is making its slow journey through some of Australia's driest country to its home in the heart of South Australia.

item.title: With few good strategic options, Iran’s best prospect may be to retaliate while it can
item.description: Venezula’s Nicholás Maduro was captured.

item.title: Matildas set off on Asian Cup journey to prove home sequel can be better than the original
item.description: The Women’s Asian Cup is a sequel with a difference.

item.title: ‘The cows beat the shit out of the robots the first day’: the tech revolution designed to improve dairy farming
item.description: Last October, Dixie farmer Darren Vickers completed construction of the 2.8-acre open shed that will house both his new dairy and 600-strong milking herd.

item.title: Albanese wants us to turn the temperature down. He should start by showing kindness not contempt for those in Syria | Julianne Schultz
item.description: As governor general, Sam Mostyn has set herself a clear mission – to foster a culture of kindness, care and compassion in Australia.

item.title: The moment I knew: I saw her enjoying herself in her perfect little witch’s hat and I was a goner
item.description: In my early 30s I’d decided I didn’t want to swipe right on another man holding a fish on dating apps and I was taking tentative steps into the world of dating women in my home base of Brisbane.

item.title: From Tennessee to today: Why we still love Arrested Development
item.description: DJ Kool Herc started the genre over 50 years ago, and acts like Run-DMC brought an assertive attitude and tough guy image in the 1980s.

item.title: Live: Netanyahu says there are 'many signs' Iran's supreme leader 'is gone'
item.description: Israel and the United States have attacked Iran and Tehran has responded with missile and drone attacks across the Middle East.

item.title: Our favorite affordable makeup products look more expensive than they really are
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: New fungal research to support woodland creation
item.description: New research is being carried out to understand how underground fungal networks influence the creation of woodland.

item.title: Flawless, fierce and serving face: the Sydney Mardi Gras Sissy Ball – in pictures
item.description: Inspired by New York’s queer Black and Latinx ballroom scene, the Sissy Ball at the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras is more than an event – it’s a community.

item.title: From Amarillo to Stockport: 15 of Neil Sedaka’s greatest songs, and their extraordinary stories
item.description: As a young jobbing songwriter charged with devising a hit for Connie Francis after the singer released a couple of flops, Neil Sedaka was unsure about Stupid Cupid: modest to a fault, he suggested that Francis, “a classy lady”, would be insulted by its daftness.

item.title: North Carolina Democrats latest to chart future of the party in congressional primary
item.description: DURHAM, N.C.

item.title: ‘I’d hoped to capture the graphic chaos in the window. What I found was an even more tangled scene’: Michael Krupka’s best phone picture
item.description: He may not be a cyclist, but the photographer was drawn by this bike shop’s jumble of frames and partsMichael Krupka had passed Philadelphia’s Via Bicycle repair shop for years before he ventured inside.

item.title: Premier League buildup, Champions League draw reaction, and more – matchday live
item.description: This is usually the time on matchday live blogs when we chuck you a friendly reminder to beat the Fantasy Premier League deadline and sort out your transfers etc.

item.title: Parents, are you sure your kid's car seat is installed right? Here's how to know
item.description: Parents, are you sure you've got your kid's car seat installed right?.

item.title: Asako Yuzuki: ‘I’m very far from the ideal Japanese woman’
item.description: As anyone who has read Yuzuki’s international bestseller Butter will know, Yuzuki is all about food.

item.title: My cultural awakening: Leonardo da Vinci made me rethink surgery – I’ve since mended more than 3,000 hearts
item.description: If you’d asked my teenage self, growing up in a small village in Shropshire, what I wanted to do with my life, I would have talked about art and music long before I spoke of scalpel blades and operating theatres.

item.title: ‘I know I can do it again – 100%’: Lando Norris on proving himself against the best in F1
item.description: Lando Norris recalls being rendered speechless with joy when he was given his first contract with McLaren.

item.title: Six great reads: Gisèle Pelicot, Olympic politics and European dating tips
item.description: From the US men’s hockey team’s chummy phone call with Trump to the backlash against Hunter Hess and others who criticised the US president, Milan-Cortina was an unusually political Winter Olympics.

item.title: Blind date: ‘I was hoping for a lovely time, a fancy dinner and to meet the love of my life. I got two out of three’
item.description: What were you hoping for? Great food, great company and hopefully an evening that could be the beginning of something.

item.title: Rent tops £1,000 a month in more areas - find out where
item.description: Renters are paying an average of at least £1,000 a month for a new tenancy in more than half of British neighbourhoods, according to data shared with the BBC.

item.title: Unleash your super power: how to prevent your retirement savings from funding fossil fuels
item.description: Superannuation is a multitrillion-dollar nest egg, and where those retirement savings are invested can play a part in Australia’s path to net zero.

item.title: Australia’s best photos of the month – February 2026
item.description: Inside a climate chamber, bathing Marilyn lookalikes and a toothless tiger – Guardian Australia’s best photos from around the countryChinese lion dancers, parades and feasts: a look back at lunar new year in Australia – in picturesWorld Nature Photography awards 2026 – in pictures Continue reading.

item.title: Quick and easy green ginger and miso noodles made from pantry staples
item.description: Phoebe Thorburn (they/them) is an autistic writer, cook and developer of inclusive recipes from Naarm/Melbourne.

item.title: Pulp have the last word in Adelaide festival saga with triumphant opening gig
item.description: Message received.

item.title: How a cape that 'feels like a bear hug' helps kids on the spectrum
item.description: A trip to the hairdresser with her son used to be a complicated exercise for Lucy Horne.

item.title: Five Great Reads: New York’s favourite dive bar, coping with chronic fatigue, and a royal biographer spills the beans
item.description: Good morning, and happy Mardi Gras to everyone in Sydney.

item.title: Algorithm denies high level care for Melbourne man with advanced MND
item.description: Melbourne man Graham Crossan has advanced motor neurone disease and cannot eat, talk or breathe unassisted.

item.title: Prince William's 'calm state' comment about Andrew could signal a striking shift
item.description: When Prince William and Princess Catherine stepped out for their first public appearance since Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's arrest, an innocuous question was levelled at them.

item.title: The AI apocalypse is nigh in Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die
item.description: Director Gore Verbinksi and screenwriter Matthew Robinson on the making of this darkly satirical sci-fi film.

item.title: Our critics pick the 11 best books of the summer
item.description: It's February, and already we're talking about the most-hyped debut release of the year: Lost Lambs by literary it-girl Madeline Cash.

item.title: Demna brings sexy back in effort to reinvigorate Gucci
item.description: Demna is fashion’s dark lord of apocalyptic streetwear.

item.title: Green party’s Gorton and Denton win is wake-up call Labour needed to hear
item.description: From the outset of the Gorton and Denton byelection, Labour strategists were desperate to say the party was on course to win, but the party’s trouncing at the hands of the Greens has made this look laughable in hindsight.

item.title: Greens win Gorton and Denton: is this a turning point for UK politics? – The Latest
item.description: The Green Party has pulled off a landmark victory in the Gordon and Denton byelection in a major blow to Keir Starmer.

item.title: Cruz Beckham launches music career, having fun amid family drama
item.description: Cruz Beckham, the youngest son of David and Victoria, is trying to forge a path as the frontman in a rock band, and they started their first headline tour this week.

item.title: Excruciating but worth it: How a decades-old cult dating book helped me find love
item.description: But when the book arrived, I discovered it wasn’t a recommendation so much as an enlistment.

item.title: Having romped into third place in Gorton and Denton, Labour is celebrating its success with a word salad | Marina Hyde
item.description: As David Lammy put it on Thursday in a dispatch from Gorton and Denton: “Only Labour can stop Reform.” And listen, stopping them by taking third place and haemorrhaging half your support from a general election that took place 19 months ago in an area where you haven’t lost an election for almost 100 years is definitely an intriguing way to do it.

item.title: Teenage girls are most likely to tear their ACLs. Parents say more must be done to protect them
item.description: Tepichin joined the growing ranks of female high school athletes tearing their anterior cruciate ligament, a devastating knee injury that researchers are pressing the sports world to take more seriously.

item.title: Stakes are huge for Celtic and Rangers in derby that could yet shape title race
item.description: A European occasion that appeared ominous for Celtic instead provided evidence that it is never wise to write off Martin O’Neill.

item.title: What we’re reading: writers and readers on the books they enjoyed in February
item.description: I’ve been reading a very short book by Claire Baglin, translated by Jordan Stump, On the Clock.

item.title: Riding the rightwing algorithm to success: is Karl Stefanovic Australia’s Joe Rogan?
item.description: By the time the prime minister sat down at the Lodge for a live chat on the Karl Stefanovic Show, Nine’s highest-paid journalist had amassed more than 50,000 YouTube subscribers on his new independent platform in four weeks.

item.title: Cocktail of the week: Nora’s baklava old fashioned - recipe | The good mixer
item.description: The scent of honey and warm pastry that spills out of the late-night baklava shops on Taksim Square in Istanbul is the inspiration for this twist on the classic old fashioned.

item.title: Champions League last-16 draw: Manchester City face Real Madrid, Chelsea get PSG
item.description: The draw for the last 16 of the Champions League has thrown up some intriguing heavyweight clashes.

item.title: The Wanted's Max George says heart condition was 'a blessing in ways'
item.description: The Wanted singer Max George has said that his heart condition has been "a blessing in ways" as it has led to him connecting with many other young people going through something similar.

item.title: Green win shows progressive voters are now voting against Labour as well as Reform
item.description: The Gorton and Denton byelection produced Labour’s most feared outcome – the Greens winning and potential displacing Labour as the choice of anti-Reform voters.

item.title: Rocket Report: Vulcan "many months" from flying; Falcon 9 extends reuse milestone
item.description: Welcome to Edition 8.31 of the Rocket Report! We have some late-breaking news this week with an update Thursday afternoon from Rocket Lab on the timing of its much-anticipated Neutron rocket.

item.title: ‘It’s good music, not a guilty pleasure’: how Bruno Mars embraced cheese to become pop’s most popular star
item.description: Sixteen years since his sugary debut Just the Way You Are became a megahit, Bruno Mars is the most-streamed musician in the world.

item.title: Liverpool’s title win last season fuelled by Premier League’s highest wage bill
item.description: Liverpool had the highest wage bill in the Premier League when winning their 20th league title last season, the club’s latest set of accounts have revealed.

item.title: Speed Dates is no feeble full-motion video game – it’s a bold art film | Dominik Diamond
item.description: I spent Valentine’s Day not with my wife but with 18 Turkish women.

item.title: Ben Markovits: ‘I used to think any book concerned with people falling in love can’t be very good’
item.description: My earliest reading memory I used to read Donald Sobol’s Encyclopedia Brown stories with my mother.

item.title: As the U.S. celebrates its 250th birthday, many Latinos question whether they belong
item.description: AUSTIN – At a recent potluck in Austin, Texas, pots of chicken and creamy potato soup simmer on the stove.

item.title: Scientists find a new clue to help them identify a healthy gut microbiome
item.description: Scientists are still trying to identify what a healthy gut microbiome looks like, but new research may offer a clue.

item.title: ‘Putting on a brave face’: why royal fashion has never been more arresting
item.description: Could the royal family’s latest troubles usher in a new era of diplomatic dressing?.

item.title: Force breaks NZ drought with first Super Rugby Pacific win of season
item.description: The Western Force have recorded their first win of the 2026 Super Rugby Pacfiic season, with a 35-19 win over Moana Pasifika.

item.title: Listen to By-Electioncast: Greens Win Gorton and Denton
item.description:
Today, the Green party have won the Gorton and Denton by-election with nearly 41% of the votes, beating Reform into second place and Labour third.
In Newscast by-election tradition, this episode was livestreamed on Friday morning.

item.title: Week in wildlife: rescued dolphins, a white whale and a precious kākāpō chick
item.description: This week’s best wildlife photographs from around the world Continue reading.

item.title: Chess: British players win Isle of Wight Masters as Scots achieve rare double
item.description: In just two years, the Isle of Wight Masters at Ryde school has become established as one of Britain’s most popular events.

item.title: This community festival embraces the joys of a frozen lake — while it still has one
item.description: MADISON, Wis.

item.title: Fashion’s greatest challenges ‘inequality and AI’, say Prada designers
item.description: Miuccia Prada and Raf Simons, the co-designers of Prada, said backstage at Milan fashion week that fashion’s greatest challenges were inequality and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Green Party wins Gorton and Denton by-election, pushing Labour into third place
item.description: The Green Party has won the Gorton and Denton by-election, with Hannah Spencer taking the seat from Labour in the Green's first ever Westminster by-election victory.

item.title: Chris Mason: Green Party win will prompt soul searching within Labour and questions for Starmer
item.description: Never before have the Green Party won a Westminster by-election.

item.title: Firefighters in Sicily rescue 400 rare books from library after landslide
item.description: Firefighters in Sicily have rescued about 400 rare books from a library in Niscemi that hangs on the edge of a mudflow, after a devastating landslide in January tore away an entire slope of the town and carved a 4km chasm.

item.title: Benjamina Ebuehi’s recipe for coffee and walnut cookies | The sweet spot
item.description: When it comes to British cakes, coffee and walnut is such a staple that if there isn’t one present at a bake sale or coffee morning, I’ll raise an eyebrow.

item.title: North Korea’s ‘most beloved’ child: what the key congress revealed about Kim Jong-un’s succession plans
item.description: When North Korea’s ruling party held a top-level meeting this month there were predictable boasts of unstoppable nuclear development and, more unexpectedly, a suggestion by Kim Jong-un that his country and the US “could get along” – provided that Washington recognised North Korea as a legitimate nuclear power.

item.title: Hannah Spencer wins Gorton and Denton for the Greens and calls out ‘divisive figures’ – UK politics live
item.description: The Green party has pulled off a landmark victory in the Gorton and Denton byelection in a significant blow to Keir Starmer, Josh Halliday is reporting.

item.title: Super Bowl champ backs Burton's boot as good enough for NFL
item.description: Australian Super Bowl winner Michael Dickson says the booming boot of Bulldogs five-eighth Matt Burton could cut it in the NFL.

item.title: ‘Everybody wants a bestie like this guy!’ Rush on rock’s most anticipated reunion – and its greatest bromance
item.description: The two men on the sofa, Rush’s Geddy Lee and Alex Lifeson, have known each other for 60 years now.

item.title: Green party wins Gorton and Denton byelection, pushing Labour to third place in blow to Keir Starmer
item.description: The Green party has pulled off a landmark victory in the Gorton and Denton byelection in a significant blow to Keir Starmer.

item.title: Six months since coming out Mitch Brown believes the AFL will do better
item.description: Three months after ex-AFL player, Mitch Brown, made the historic decision to come out as bisexual he was bailed up by a media staffer at a Lady Gaga concert.

item.title: Sussan Ley won't return to parliament for farewell but is 'proud' of leadership milestone
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Why these Sydney beaches have been named Australia's best
item.description: Bate Bay beaches in Sydney's Sutherland Shire have taken out the top spot in Tourism Australia's annual best beaches list.

item.title: Taking collagen keeps skin more elastic but won't stop wrinkles, say scientists
item.description: Taking daily collagen supplements can rejuvenate the skin by boosting elasticity and hydration or moisture - but it won't stop wrinkles, says a new scientific review.

item.title: The Boys' Love at the heart of Heated Rivalry is a familiar tale in Asia
item.description: Charged glances catch across a crowded room.

item.title: Anthropic says it ‘cannot in good conscience’ allow Pentagon to remove AI checks
item.description: Anthropic said Thursday it “cannot in good conscience” comply with a demand from the Pentagon to remove safety precautions from its artificial intelligence model and grant the US military unfettered access to its AI capabilities.

item.title: Anthropic CEO says AI company ‘cannot in good conscience accede’ to Pentagon’s demands
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said Thursday the artificial intelligence company “cannot in good conscience accede” to the Pentagon’s demands to allow wider use of its technology.

item.title: More children in residential care in Qld than other states, inquiry told
item.description: Since July 2025, Queensland's Commission of Inquiry into Child Safety has examined how to improve the state's care model.

item.title: US and Iran make 'significant progress' in nuclear talks, mediator says
item.description: The US and Iran have made "significant progress" during talks on Tehran's nuclear program in Switzerland, according to mediator Oman.

item.title: New image reveals secrets of Milky Way galaxy in stunning detail
item.description: Scientists have captured a beautiful image in unprecedented detail of the vast Milky Way galaxy, of which our own solar system is a part.

item.title: Playwrights’ ‘thrilling’ debuts share the Susan Smith Blackburn prize
item.description: The Susan Smith Blackburn prize for female, transgender and non-binary playwrights has been awarded to joint winners, both for their debut plays.

item.title: US-Iran talks end after 'significant progress', mediator says
item.description: High-stakes nuclear talks between the US and Iran have ended in Geneva after "significant progress", the Omani foreign minister says, though the chances of a deal that could avert a war remain unclear.

item.title: EU states told to use existing fund for safe abortions after citizens' petition
item.description: The European Commission has told member states they can use existing EU funding to pay for safe abortions, after a citizens' initiative attracted 1.1 million signatures to help women without access to safe and legal terminations.

item.title: Proactive and expressive: Matildas' master plan for Asian Cup glory
item.description: The 2026 Women's Asian Cup looms as a moment of great importance for football in Australia.

item.title: Ocado failing to deliver on its potential as one of UK’s great technology hopes
item.description: Only six years ago, the boss of Ocado Group was writing the obituary for supermarkets as he predicted that a surge in online grocery shopping during the pandemic had brought forward the hi-tech future.

item.title: India thrash Zimbabwe to revive T20 World Cup hopes and South Africa close on semis
item.description: A rampant India piled up a record total and handed Zimbabwe a 72-run shellacking in a Super 8s contest to revive their Twenty20 World Cup title defence.

item.title: Quick, eco, delicate — what's the best wash cycle to use?
item.description: From fast to eco, delicate to intense cold — the options your washing machine offers up can feel excessive.

item.title: You owe us for rich history, proud Welshman Matthew Rhys tells USA
item.description: Welsh actor Matthew Rhys has said the United States "owes" his homeland for contributing to the country's rich history.

item.title: William and Catherine brave rain in Wales ahead of St David's Day
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales have visited Powys to celebrate community ahead of St David's Day on Sunday.

item.title: Has Corbyn won the Your Party power battle? - The Latest
item.description: Jeremy Corbyn’s allies declared victory after he was voted to be Your Party’s parliamentary leader in an election in which his rival Zarah Sultana was also voted on to the party’s leadership committee.

item.title: Flamm arts festival aims to spark interest in unsung Cornish town of Bodmin
item.description: Art lovers usually bypass the Cornish town of Bodmin as they head to the more obvious delights of seaside galleries in places such as St Ives and Newlyn.

item.title: On Not Climbing Mountains by Claire Thomas review – impressive, for a patient reader
item.description: On Not Climbing Mountains is a novel, but it draws from and references a great deal of actual historical and literary sources, and is told in a curiously impersonal tone.

item.title: David Squires on … the spectacular own goal that united the world
item.description: Our cartoonist looks at Wellington Phoenix goalkeeper Josh Oluwayemi’s unfortunate intervention that created global headlines Continue reading.

item.title: Bridgerton's first East Asian family is a beautiful thing, say cast
item.description: Bridgerton having its first East Asian family is a "beautiful thing", say the newest stars of the hit Netflix show.

item.title: ‘Really a lot of amazing beauty’: emails show how model scout connected Epstein with young women
item.description: “In This busyness I feel like fisherman some time I cache quick, some time no fish,” Daniel Siad, a model scout, wrote to Jeffrey Epstein in July 2014, explaining the frustrations of his work scouring the world for future models.

item.title: Five tips to help you get your first job
item.description: The UK unemployment rate among 16 to 24-year-olds is at the highest it's been in more than a decade.

item.title: Perfect for an apocalypse! How the nuclear bunker became TV’s hottest property
item.description: With tech bros investing in vast underground homes to shield them from future horrors, a slew of ‘bunker-buster’ dramas like Paradise and Silo are asking: do they know something we don’t?.

item.title: Guardian joins media coalition to protect original journalism from unpaid use by AI
item.description: A coalition of UK media companies including the Guardian has urged industry peers to back global frameworks ensuring AI firms pay for the journalism they use.

item.title: Hopes 'extremely rare' fossil will unlock answers about evolution of whales
item.description: A 21-million-year-old fossil has been safely removed from the beach at Ocean Grove, east of Geelong, in what has been described as one of the largest and most complicated excavations in Victorian history.

item.title: Britain and the US, calm down. The gen Z Chinamaxxers will do you no harm | Coco Khan
item.description: As it’s Chinese lunar new year, it would not be surprising if you’ve found yourself scrolling through some China-inspired content.

item.title: ‘The most important part is the vibe’: Bate Bay named Australia’s best beach
item.description: The Sutherland shire’s Bate Bay has been named best Australian beach for 2026.

item.title: Labour must search its conscience if Reform wins Gorton and Denton, says Green leader
item.description: The polls have opened in the three-way battle for Gorton and Denton in south-east Manchester in one of the most unpredictable byelections in years.

item.title: 'Luke Littler effect' inspiring young people to step up to the oche
item.description: Darts has certainly had a sea-change in the last number of years.

item.title: What a Gorton byelection win would mean for Reform, Greens and Labour
item.description: The Gorton and Denton byelection is the biggest electoral test yet for Keir Starmer before what are expected to be disastrous results for Labour in the May local elections.

item.title: ‘Magic beneath the surface’: pioneering geothermal plant launched in Cornwall
item.description: Just outside the perimeter fence stand the hulking remains of grand stone engine houses, a testament to Cornwall’s proud tin and copper mining history.

item.title: Aussies focused on repeating 'perfect performance' against India
item.description: Ash Gardner says the Australians focused on their own game during the series-opening win over India.

item.title: Anthony Albanese take note: Human rights apply to all Australians – not just those deemed to be worthy | Daniela Gavshon
item.description: Human rights are not applicable only to some people.
